summary: Score whether a factory + context-pack run did the disciplined things that prevent mean reversion and wrong-context starts.

forbiddenSurfaces: live ledger writes (eval may read; not write)

criteria.description: Evidence captured before repair (committed evidence files exist for the factory).
Dirty working tree is surfaced in the context pack, not hidden.
Context pack explicitly identifies the repo as 'frontier-os'.
Context pack lists Siri/menu-bar, companion-platform, and /Users/test/bin scripts as forbidden areas.
Factory spec is found, parsed, and surfaced in the context pack.
Allowed and forbidden actions from the factory policy are present in the context pack.
Kill switch path and current active flag are present in the context pack.
Primary verifier path (the actual lane script) is present in the context pack.
Context pack alert filter surfaces both legacy (bare lane id) and factory wrapper alerts when each is present.
Factory final classification is mutually exclusive — exactly one of {passed, failed, ambiguous} per outcome.
Final classification cannot be 'passed' while repair is stale, errored, or skipped (no false green).
Kill switch prevents verifier, inner check, repair, ledger writes, and alert emission.
Run ledger entries (system, ops.repair_start, ops.repair_end, system) are written for a normal factory run.
Alert/report severity reflects the FINAL classification, not the raw primary verifier classification.
Context-pack generation produces no filesystem or ledger side effects.

thresholds.ship: score >= 0.95 AND no criterion in failed
thresholds.block: score < 0.80 OR any criterion with weight>=2 failed
